summary: AI user-simulation testing for production agents and GenAI apps.
editor_note: AI QA should simulate customers, not checklist prompts. Arato generates simulation context, scenarios, multi-turn interactions, and turn-level analysis across text, voice, data, and image. Unlike model eval libraries, it starts from the product surface and business goals. The risk is whether outside-in tests catch failures before real users do.

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes simulation context, generated scenarios, simulated user interactions, and analysis for validating AI apps.
The product copy says Arato can run multi-modal user journeys through the actual interface across text, voice, data, and images.
The site describes Arato Observe, Arato Studio, APIs, SDKs, A/B testing, custom evals, session replay, and experiment audit trails.

market_context.primary_user: Product, QA, AI engineering, and compliance teams shipping customer-facing AI agents.
market_context.differentiation: Arato tests agents by simulating user behavior against the product experience instead of only scoring model outputs in offline eval sets.
market_context.why_now: More companies are putting agents into customer workflows, making repeatable user-simulation tests more useful than prompt checklists.

risks.claim: The falsifiable risk is whether outside-in simulations catch the high-impact production failures that real users discover first.
risks.basis: VentureDex assessment from the official product site and seed funding coverage.
